IEEE TRANSACTIONS ON ENGINEERING MANAGEMENT, VOL. 72, 2025
An Efﬁciency-Uncertainty-Based Consensus
Optimization Framework With Dual-Perspective
Clustering Analysis for Blockchain Adoption
in Supply Chains
Ardavan Babaei
, Erfan Babaee Tirkolaee
, Sadia Samar Ali
, Sankar Kumar Roy
,
and Gerhard-Wilhelm Weber
Abstract—The integration of blockchain technology (BT) in sup-
ply chains (SCs) is advancing rapidly due to its beneﬁts in trans-
parency, traceability, security, and decentralization. However, SC
managers face challenges in evaluating diverse strategies for effec-
tive BT adoption. This study proposes a comprehensive framework
combining criteria weighting and evaluation models that prioritize
simplicity, idealism, structure, and efﬁciency. It integrates ﬁve
decision-making techniques; i.e., simple additive weighted (SAW),
entropy method (EM), technique for order of preference by sim-
ilarity to ideal solution (TOPSIS), super-efﬁciency data envelop-
ment analysis (SEDEA), and network data envelopment analysis
(NDEA), to provide a dynamic, adaptable evaluation process. In
addition, a novel risk- and scenario-based optimization model
and a predictive comparison approach are introduced to enhance
decision-making. The framework is applied to the Norwegian Oil
and Gas (O&G) sector, analyzing four BT adoption strategies. It
incorporates machine learning (ML) for similarity analysis and
dual-perspective clustering to better understand inter-strategy and
method relationships. Findings show that a focused, single-purpose
blockchain use, especially under high-risk conditions and trans-
formational strategies, is most effective. Key adoption drivers

include market and customer pressures, collaboration needs, and
limited IT infrastructure. Simplicity and idealism/nonidealism-
based models aligned most closely with existing literature, while
network structure-based models varied more. The results demon-
strate the importance of consensus-based evaluation when selecting
blockchain strategies in SCs.
Managerial Relevance Statement—This study provides engineer-
ing managers and policy-makers with a practical, robust and
evidence-based framework to evaluate and select blockchain im-
plementation strategies in the Oil and Gas Supply Chain (OGSC)
sector, even under complex operational conditions and diverse
stakeholder demands. By integrating multiple decision-making
models and a consensus-based optimization method, the frame-
work enables systematic, comparative, and risk-adjusted strategy
assessment. Scenariowise aggregation and dual-perspective cluster
analysis using machine learning (ML) clarify similarities among
alternativesandevaluationmodels,supportinginformeddifferenti-
ation and prioritization of strategies. Findings highlight that widely
assumed “transformational” strategies are not always optimal;
under high-risk conditions, targeted or single-use strategies may
deliver greater efﬁciency gains and risk mitigation. The framework
offers a step-by-step operational roadmap from identifying busi-
ness needs to model-based evaluation, ranking, scenario analysis,
and consensus-building, equipping managers and policy-makers
with context-sensitive tools to improve supply chain efﬁciency,
transparency, and strategic decision-making.
Index Terms—Blockchain technology (BT), consensus, data env-
elopment analysis (DEA), decision-making, supply chain manag-
ement (SCM).
I. INTRODUCTION
A. Blockchain Technology
A
CCORDING to a report by Fortune Business Insights [46],
the global blockchain technology (BT) market was valued
at USD 27.85 billion in 2024 and is projected to grow signiﬁ-
cantly, reaching USD 746.41 billion by 2032, with a compound
annual growth rate of 49.7%. This substantial growth is driven by
increasing digital transformation, rising cybersecurity threats,
and expanding applications across industries such as banking,
ﬁnancial services and insurance, healthcare, and supply chain
management (SCM). Blockchain is an append-only database
thatisdecentralized,transparent,immutable,resistanttotamper-
ing, and operates without the need for trust [1]. BT, characterized

BABAEI et al.: EFFICIENCY-UNCERTAINTY-BASED CONSENSUS OPTIMIZATION FRAMEWORK WITH DUAL-PERSPECTIVE
4309
by a permissioned distributed ledger for storing transaction
data, holds promise for the oil and natural gas industry due to
its secure data handling within a permissioned network. This
industry, reliant on extensive data for operations such as asset
history, land management, and ﬁnancial records, can beneﬁt
from blockchain’s ability to ensure operations adhere to prede-
ﬁned parameters and securely store process veriﬁcation data in
a distributed ledger [2]. In addition, blockchain offers potential
improvements in transparency and accuracy for freight rates,
shipment routing, and invoice generation, thereby enhancing
SCM, expediting processes, and reducing transactional fraud.
Furthermore, blockchain can facilitate the complexities of com-
modity trading on oil platforms, allowing for the development
of trade ﬁnance platforms to facilitate crude oil transactions
among trading parties. The Oil and Gas (O&G) industry faces
signiﬁcant challenges, including extensive paperwork and rec-
onciliation processes that escalate both monetary and time ex-
pendituresassociatedwithtransactions.Furthermore,multiparty
investment and cooperation in the industry heighten the risks of
fraud, errors, and inefﬁciencies in transactions. Moreover, the
costs associated with third-party management are signiﬁcantly
high, along with inefﬁcient negotiations regarding trade and the
slow exchange of essential information. Moreover, there is an
increased risk of cyber-attacks targeting critical industry data.
Consequently, the O&G sector urgently requires a reformation
of its management strategies [3].
BT, a relatively novel innovation, has emerged as a promis-
ing solution. Since bitcoin’s introduction in 2008 [4], BT has
evolved markedly over the last ten years. It has moved through
various phases: the initial stage dominated by bitcoin, the second
phase characterized by Ethereum and self-executing contracts,
and the present third stage emphasizing social platforms, all
illustrating the development of BT.
B. Blockchain Utilization in the O&G and Petroleum
Industries
Despite its broad implementation in diverse sectors, the
O&G sector initially approached blockchain cautiously and
largely refrained from active involvement. However, with British
Petroleum (BP) initiating blockchain trials in 2017, the indus-
try took its primary steps toward integrating BT. Coordinating
SCs in the petroleum sector plays a key role in driving global
expansion and inﬂuence. However, conventional petroleum SC
encounters difﬁculties such as intricate extraction procedures,
reﬁning obstacles, distribution challenges, environmental harm,
and problems with product traceability. Zhou et al. [5] suggested
a blockchain-based product evaluation management system us-
ing the interplanetary ﬁle system to store evaluation data and
smart contracts on Ethereum for retrieval. The system ensured
data integrity, reliable retrieval, and sustainability through eco-
nomic incentives, demonstrating usability, reliability, and sus-
tainability in experimental results.
Advances in technology offer opportunities to transform the
petroleum SC, with blockchain and Internet of Things (IoT)
sensors enabling greater transparency and security [6]. Müller
et al. [7] investigated the Industry 4.0 adoption of small and
medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), highlighting challenges in
integrating technologies horizontally, vertically, and end-to-end.
Usingmultiplecasestudies,theyidentiﬁedbarriersandenablers,
proposing an integration framework. Findings emphasized
operational beneﬁts over strategic gains, relying on stakeholder
collaboration. Recently, BT has progressed remarkably, becom-
ing a signiﬁcant trend across multiple industries. Initially ap-
proached with caution in the petroleum sector, technologies such
as fog computing, IoT, and cloud computing, combined with
blockchain, present vast opportunities to improve operational
efﬁciency. For instance, Gazprom Neft, a leading Russian oil
company, has demonstrated that digital technologies can en-
hance productivity in the sector by 10%–15% [8]. The trading of
petroleum products such as gasoline and diesel necessitates high
levelsofsecurity,conﬁdentiality,andefﬁcientdatamanagement.
However, current systems for managing petroleum trade are
oftencentralized,untransparent,andunreliable.Inthepetroleum
industry, external factors greatly affect SCM, highlighting the
need for a thorough understanding and ongoing improvement
efforts [9]. Given the crucial role of the petroleum sector in
the global economy to satisfy high-volume energy demands,
digitalization is becoming progressively necessary in all stages,
including extraction, reﬁning, marketing, and distribution.
In the dynamic context of petroleum SCM, ensuring product
integrity during transport presents inherent risks in traditional
centralized systems. However, the rise of BT marks a signif-
icant transformation, rendering a decentralized approach that
enhances transparency and accountability [10]. This innovative
technology addresses the intricacies of global SCs, paving the
way for improved transparency, traceability, and security [11].
Researchers have been also investigating blockchain’s diverse
applications in SCM and sustainability projects, and overcoming
implementation challenges, which in turn are revolutionizing
digital currency functions and alleviating concerns related to
cost, storage, and security [12], [13]. As a form of distributed
ledger technology (DLT), blockchain consists of a sophisti-
cated network of linked, time-stamped records safeguarded by
cryptographic techniques. Its decentralized structure enables
consensus and validation across the network of nodes, ensuring a
secure, unchangeable, and trustless transaction framework [14].
Beyond traditional business functions, blockchain eliminates
the need for intermediaries, acting as a distributed database.
Known for maintaining transactional conﬁdentiality, integrity,
and availability, blockchain functions as a shared, transparent
ledger across a peer-to-peer network. Data and transactions
are securely recorded and veriﬁed cryptographically, ensuring
immutability once added and requiring majority consensus for
any changes [15].
In summary, the O&G industry is increasingly recognizing
the value of BT, particularly when integrated with advanced
technologies such as IoT and AI. This study explores the ap-
plication of BT in the petroleum sector, focusing on its role
in decision-making and SC optimization. By addressing exist-
ing gaps and introducing innovative frameworks, the aim is
to provide actionable insights for industry leaders, laying the
foundations for a more efﬁcient, secure, and sustainable O&G
industry.

C. Research Questions
RQ1: How can blockchain strategies be effectively evaluated for
the OGSC despite its complexity and limited prior research?
RQ2: What comprehensive evaluation framework can be devel-
oped to investigate blockchain deployment strategies in the
O&Gsector,incorporatingmultipledecision-makingphiloso-
phies?
RQ3: How can evaluation results from different decision-
making methodologies be consolidated to provide an optimal
uniﬁed perspective for OGSC managers?
RQ4: What policy implications, particularly risk, statistics, and
scenario-basedapproaches,shouldbeconsideredwhenaggre-
gating evaluation results for BT adoption in the O&G sector?
RQ5: How do novel concepts (e.g., prediction-based approaches
for comparing decision-making methods) enhance the eval-
uation of BT adoption strategies compared to traditional
methods?
D. Research Objectives
1) Evaluating Blockchain Deployment Strategies: This re-
search assesses various BT adoption strategies for using
blockchain in the O&G industry to understand their efﬁ-
cacy and performance,
2) Employing Different Decision-Making Philosophies: We
apply network decision-making and data-driven mod-
els based on simplicity, idealism, and nonidealism
philosophies, and super-efﬁciency concepts to evaluate
blockchain deployment strategies comprehensively,
3) Developing a Consolidation Method: An optimization
using mathematical programming techniques is developed
to consolidate evaluation results obtained from different
decision-making approaches to provide SC managers with
a uniﬁed perspective,
4) Addressing Policy Implications: We ﬁnally investigate and
present policies for SC managers regarding the aggrega-
tion of evaluation results, particularly focusing on risk
and scenario-based approaches. This study, for the ﬁrst
time compared to the literature, indicates the importance
of consensus from a statistical perspective on prediction,
contrasting it with traditional approaches.
E. Research Structure
The rest of this article is organized as follows. The literature
is reviewed in Section II to highlight the main contributions.
The problem is stated in Section III, where the evaluation
models are described in Section IV. Section V represents the
obtained results, which are then discussed in Section VI. Finally,
Section VII concludes the article and offers a useful outlook for
future studies.
II. LITERATURE REVIEW
Here, the research works addressing the adoption BT in SCs
with the help of multiattribute decision-making and mathe-
matical optimization methods are critically reviewed. As an
important point, efforts to integrate BT into optimization and
data-driven models in the petroleum sector are still in the early
stages. Babaei et al. [16] introduced a dual-objective optimiza-
tion model incorporating blockchain’s clarity into a sophisti-
cated three-level SCM system. Using various algorithms, they
aimed to evaluate essential factors such as expenses, service
quality, and environmental impact [17], [18]. To meet the indus-
try’s evolving demands, Babaei et al. [19] offered an ecofriendly
circular SCM system enhanced with blockchain transparency.
In addition, multiobjective optimization frameworks were also
developed to address challenges related to cost-effectiveness,
trafﬁc congestion, and openness [20]. Sharma et al. [21] inves-
tigated BT adoption in the agriculture SC, comparing trends
in developed and developing economies. Using data from The
Netherlands, United States, Saudi Arabia, and India, they spec-
iﬁed enabling factors and barriers, highlighting policies as the
key enabler.
Cutting-edge research investigates synchromodal transporta-
tion strategies combined with BT, aiming to align with envi-
ronmental sustainability goals. Oudani et al. [22] proposed an
environmentally friendly blockchain model aimed at improving
energy efﬁciency in the management of hazardous materials
within SCs. Similarly, Carvalho et al. [23] crafted an extensive
model dedicated to reﬁning strategies for blockchain integration
and the design of SCM systems. Munim et al. [24] applied a
strategic decision-making model to assess BT adoption within
the oil industry, fostering novel methodologies. Babaei et al.
[25] emphasized the signiﬁcant impact of risk and uncertainty
on decision-making regarding new technologies in SCs. Their
study examined how blockchain strategies can mitigate these
challenges, emphasizing optimization models tailored to man-
age SC dynamics and technological uncertainties, but it does
notintegratedecision-makingmethodsorconsolidateevaluation
results. Youseﬁand Tosarkani [26] performed a thorough study
to pinpoint and rank the factors that facilitate blockchain imple-
mentation in the mineral SC industry. Lotﬁet al. [27] explored
the adoption of resilient and sustainable SC network designs
powered by BT. Kumar et al. [28] examined the signiﬁcance of
Industry 4.0 in revolutionizing industries, emphasizing the dig-
ital food SCM system’s role. They suggested some frameworks
to evaluate system performance, optimizing blockchain-speciﬁc
metrics such as latency, throughput, and round-trip time for
improved outcomes. Furthermore, Goli [29] utilized a mathe-
matical approach to enhance the design of product portfolios
and manage ﬁnancial transactions within circular SCM systems
by adopting BT. Similarly, Rahmanzadeh et al. [30] introduced
a strategic model for SC planning that incorporates open inno-
vation. They employed a fuzzy logic method to reﬁne decision-
makingprocessesinalignmentwithbothSCgoalsandprinciples
of open innovation. They also suggested a blockchain-driven
registration system to tackle intellectual property issues.
Recently, Abdel-Basset et al. [31] developed a multi-criteria
decision-making (MCDM) framework based on the analytical
hierarchy process and technique for order of preference by
similarity to ideal solution (TOPSIS) to assess the effect of
technologies of Industry 5.0. It was revealed that blockchain,
5G, drones, nano-technology, Big Data, robotics, IoT, cloud
computing, and artiﬁcial intelligence (AI) play key roles.

Ye et al. [32] investigated the adoption of BT in agri-food SCs,
evaluating beneﬁts and costs. They explored conditions under
which competing SCs beneﬁt from BT adoption and proposed
optimal government subsidy schemes. The obtained results
highlighted competitive dynamics and the need for subsidies
to enhance consumer surplus and social welfare. Gardas et al.
[33] presented a novel node selection procedure for blockchain-
enabled edge IoT systems, addressing cloud-based IoT issues.
Using fuzzy logic and TOPSIS, their framework enhanced node
selection, improving response times, resource allocation, and
data trust. Büyüközkan and Tüfekci [34] applied a decision-
making process using the VIseKriterijumska Optimizacija I
Kompromisno Resenje (VIKOR) method to appraise enterprise
blockchain platforms. It combined multiple preference formats,
aiding organizations in selecting the most suitable blockchain
platform, thus enhancing blockchain literature. They concluded
that as blockchain platforms are increasingly utilized in diverse
business applications, evaluating them becomes crucial. Lai and
Liao [35] offered a hybrid methodology based on linguistic D
numbers, double normalization-based multiple aggregation, and
criteria importance through intercriteria correlation technique to
address information uncertainty and criteria correlations. They
evaluated information uncertainty, variety of criteria, and the
correlations between criteria in a numerical example. Filatovas
et al. [36] suggested a framework for choosing suitable con-
sensus protocols for DLTs using MCDM techniques. They ad-
dressed varying requirements, demonstrating their effectiveness
with common blockchain systems and bike-renting applications.
Erol et al. [37] emphasized the growing importance of sus-
tainable development in building resilient SCs. Their proposed
approach was based on an integrated fuzzy MCDM model to
assess blockchain’s applicability in sustainable SCs, identifying
sourcing, delivery, transformation, and product recovery as the
most suitable functions. Dehshiri et al. [38] addressed renewable
energy development challenges by proposing blockchain strate-
gies to mitigate risks. With the help of strengths, weaknesses,
opportunities, and threats analysis and gray MCDM methods,
they demonstrated blockchain’s potential to decentralize the
energy system, reducing renewable energy production costs and
prices. Kara et al. [39] developed a decision support system for
selecting blockchain platforms for digital projects. Employing
MCDM and interval-valued spherical fuzzy sets, they examined
a case study along with an extensive sensitivity analysis. Babaei
et al. [47] proposed a decision support framework to evaluate
blockchain deployment in the OGSC, focusing on cost, proﬁt,
and income efﬁciency using DEA models. Applied to Norway’s
O&G sector, the results showed varying effectiveness of strate-
gies under deterministic and uncertain conditions. The literature
review highlights the growing interest in BT adoption across
various industries, including SCs. Table D.1 in the Supplemen-
tary Material summarizes key contributions from the literature,
identiﬁes research gaps, and outlines how our study advances
the ﬁeld.
To address the critical need for a robust research method-
ology, our study presents and applies a comprehensive, multi-
faceted approach to evaluating BT adoption strategies within
SCs. The methodology is based on a combination of established
decision-making models; i.e., entropy method (EM), TOPSIS,
simple additive weighted (SAW), and data envelopment analysis
(DEA) models, each selected for its distinctive ability to offer
insightful and complementary perspectives on the data. By inte-
grating these models, our approach facilitates a thorough eval-
uation that balances simplicity and efﬁciency while providing a
holistic view. The EM, for instance, objectively determines the
importance of criteria based on data variability, while TOPSIS
ranks options by their proximity to an ideal solution. SAW offers
a straightforward approach for initial assessments, and DEA
conducts an in-depth efﬁciency analysis across multiple inputs
and outputs. This layered methodology not only strengthens the
comprehensiveness of our ﬁndings but also enables a compre-
hensive understanding of blockchain implementation strategies
within SCs. Our ﬁndings are further validated through practical
application in the Norwegian O&G sector, demonstrating the
efﬁcacy of our methodology in real-world scenarios. In accor-
dance with the literature review and introduction, the research
contributions can be summarized as follows.
A. Contributions
1) Comprehensive Evaluation: This study delivers an in-
depth assessment of various BT adoption strategies within
the petroleum sector. By employing multiple decision-
making approaches, including SEDEA, SAW, TOPSIS,
and NDEA, we offer a comprehensive understanding of
how different strategies perform. This approach allows for
a thorough evaluation of blockchain’s impact on OGSC
optimization, addressing gaps identiﬁed in previous re-
search, such as those by Babaei et al. [16], [47] and Munim
et al. [24], who explored blockchain integration but did not
fully consolidate the results across various methodologies.
2) Utilization of Various Decision-Making: Our methodol-
ogy integrates diverse decision-making techniques, each
rooted in different theoretical philosophies; i.e., network
decision-making, data-driven models, and consensus op-
timization frameworks, to assess blockchain deployment
strategies. This multifaceted approach contributes to a
more robust evaluation framework, enhancing the under-
standing of blockchain’s role in improving SC efﬁciency
and addressing complexities that were previously studied
in isolation (e.g., [19], [34], [35]).
3) Consolidation of Evaluation Results: Introducing risk
and scenario-based policies to consolidate evaluation re-
sults from different methodologies offers a novel ap-
proach to address the need for comprehensive insights for
OGSC managers. By consolidating diverse results into a
cohesive, mathematically optimized risk-scenario-based
framework, we address the need for holistic insights that
previous studies, such as those by Babaei et al. [25],
have highlighted but not fully realized. In addition to
tackling optimization challenges, this article adopts a
dual-perspective clustering analysis informed by machine
learning (ML) to uncover similarities among strategies
and relationships between evaluation methods. Especially,
the dual perspective clustering approach can enable both

understanding the relative position and similarities be-
tween the different blockchain strategies, and shedding
light on the methodological similarities among the evalu-
ation models.
4) Implications: This research offers strategic and opera-
tional insights into BT adoption and the evaluation of
BT adoption strategies. In addition, it introduces novel
statistically-grounded concepts, including a prediction-
based framework for method comparison. In addition, this
research presents analyses of the application of blockchain
strategies in the O&G industry by integrating ML ap-
proaches with optimization and decision-making models.
These approaches were overlooked by Babaei et al. [25]
and Munim et al. [24].
B. Contributions to Engineering Management Practice and
Literature
This research is highly relevant to engineering manage-
ment and the academic literature, particularly in areas such as
technology adoption, supply chain optimization, and planning
under uncertainty. In capital-intensive sectors such as O&G,
managers face complex dilemmas with emerging technologies
such as blockchain. This study provides a robust, multimethod
framework (SAW, EM-TOPSIS, SEDEA, NDEA) to compare
blockchain implementation strategies, single-use, localization,
substitution, and transformation, addressing vagueness and con-
ﬂicting outputs common in single-method approaches. By intro-
ducing a risk-scenario-based consensus optimization model, the
framework incorporates uncertainties and varying risk appetites,
guiding managers to select the most appropriate strategy under
real-world conditions. The consensus-making approach resolves
conﬂicts across evaluation philosophies, supporting stakeholder
alignment and conﬁdent strategic choices.
In addition, a stepwise operational roadmap, from identifying
needs and criteria to model-based evaluation, scenario analy-
sis, consensus-building, and prioritization, provides practical
guidance. Dual-perspective clustering (using ML techniques)
offers nuanced insights into strategy and method similarities,
enabling a deeper understanding of strategic landscapes. The
integration of diverse decision-making philosophies within a
singleframework,alongwithavalidatedconsensusoptimization
model, ﬁlls a signiﬁcant gap in the literature by aggregating
heterogeneous evaluation outcomes into rigorous, actionable
engineering management decisions.
III. PROBLEM DESCRIPTION
Adopting blockchain in the OGSC is crucial due to its ability
to address signiﬁcant challenges, including complex extrac-
tion processes, reﬁning, distribution, environmental impact, and
traceability issues. Blockchain, combined with IoT sensors,
enhances transparency, security, and efﬁciency by offering a
decentralized, immutable ledger system. Blockchain ensures the
integrity and conﬁdentiality of petroleum products such as gaso-
line and diesel, which demand high security. This technology
streamlines operations, reduces costs, and eliminates the need
for intermediaries by providing a secure and transparent ledger.
The decentralized nature of blockchain enhances accountability
and traceability, addressing the vulnerabilities of centralized
systems. Thus, BT adoption is vital for improving opera-
tional efﬁciency and maintaining product integrity in the global
petroleum SC. BT adoption also involves alternative strategies.
However, adopting blockchain presents some challenges. In this
work, we investigate which strategy is most suitable for the O&G
industry. The focus is on decision-making, efﬁciency analysis,
and the consensus procedure. In other words, the presence of
sensitive data, involvement of multiple stakeholders, extensive
paperwork, and the complexity of transactions in the O&G in-
dustry underscore the urgent need for adopting BT within its SC.
In response, SC managers must diligently assess blockchain de-
ployment strategies using comprehensive criteria. Subsequently,
aggregating the evaluation results becomes crucial to make
a well-informed decision. Employing an aggregation method
based on the scenario approach allows for the incorporation
of uncertainty and risk factors into the decision-making and
evaluation processes of SC managers. As such, this section is
segmented into four sections, each representing distinct aspects
of this approach.
A pseudocode ﬂowchart outlining the research methodology
steps and scope is provided in Figure E.1 in the Supplementary
Material. In the remainder of this section, we describe the
blockchain strategies and the criteria needed to evaluate them.
Next, the evaluation models are outlined based on decision-
making concepts. Finally, we discuss the consensus process for
reconciling results from different decision-making models.
A. Blockchain Adoption Strategies
BT adoption can be divided into four stages based on these
dimensions: Single-use, localization, substitution, and trans-
formation. Munim et al. [24] offered these four strategic ap-
proaches, delineated by the interplay of complexity and novelty.
First, the “single-use” strategy entails minimal novelty and
coordination, rendering it ideal for small-scale experimentation.
In contrast, the “localization” strategy embraces high levels
of novelty but requires relatively low coordination, facilitating
streamlined operations within localized networks while aug-
menting transparency. The third strategy, termed “substitution,”
necessitates considerable coordination and relies upon estab-
lished applications, albeit with cautionary advisories regarding
potential challenges. Finally, the “transformation” strategy man-
dates heightened levels of coordination and novelty, denoting a
signiﬁcant paradigm shift within systems. As depicted in Fig. 1,
these blockchain strategies provide a comprehensive framework
for navigating the complexities of SC integration with BT.
B. Criteria for Assessing Blockchain Adoption Strategies
AccordingtoMunimetal.[24], theadoptionofBTwithinSCs
is inﬂuenced by a range of factors, both motivating and inhibit-
ing. Several factors drive the adoption of BT in industries such
as O&G. These include the necessity for collaborative efforts
to optimize technology use, consumer demand for sustainable
products driving transparency and credibility in SCs, and market

Regarding alterations, since Munim et al. [24] identiﬁed the
transformation strategy as the optimal choice, we interchange
the disagreement values between the “single-use” and “trans-
formation” strategies (for Ranks 1 and 4). In addition, aligning
with the report given by Munim et al. [24] and the NDEA
model’s preference for the transformation strategy, we substitute
the values corresponding to the second and third ranks of the
substitution strategy with those of the localization strategy.
Introducing another mutation, we combine the preceding two
mutations, resulting in four distinct scenarios: one rooted in
Table II outcomes, while the other three stem from mutations.
The outcomes of the comprehensive evaluation are presented
in Fig. 5. It is evident that, according to the second approach,
the “single-use” strategy rises as the most suitable, whereas the
transformation strategy prevails under the ﬁrst approach. It is
noteworthy that the disparity in consensus results stems from
the differing perspectives of the approaches: The ﬁrst approach
aligns with a uniﬁed viewpoint on the scenarios, whereas the
second approach seeks to mitigate the risks associated with the
scenarios to a greater extent. Hence, SC managers are advised
to select the optimal outcome based on their perspective and the
internal as well as external environment of the organization.
To analyze the strategies for blockchain adoption using the
four methods (SEDEA, SAW, TOPSIS, and NDEA) and com-
pare them to the results of Munim et al. [24], according to
Table III, we discuss the R-squared (R2) and adjusted R2 values.
These values help us understand the extent to which the results
from each method align with the results obtained by Munim
et al. [24]. Here, R2 represents the proportion of variance in
the dependent variable (Y, results from Munim et al. [24]) that
can be predicted from the independent variable (X, results from
each method). A value closer to 1 indicates a strong predictive
relationship. Adjusted R2 modiﬁes the R2 value by accounting
for the number of predictors in the model, providing a more
accurate measure of goodness-of-ﬁt, especially in models with
multiple predictors. Like R2, a value closer to 1 signiﬁes a better
model ﬁt. The application of ML for similarity analysis and
dual-perspective clustering is presented in the Supplementary
Material (Section C) in detail to better understand interstrategy
and method relationships.
VI. DISCUSSION
BT is being actively adopted by major O&G companies such
as TotalEnergies, Aramco, and Shell to enhance SC coordina-
tion, optimize operations, and reduce environmental impact.
The discussion highlights the adoption of BT by companies
in the O&G industry, showcasing its practical applications. It
explores strategies such as “single-use,” “localization,” “sub-
stitution,” and “transformation,” and presents decision-making
models of EM, TOPSIS, SAW, and DEA to guide effective
implementation. Challenges in BT adoption are acknowledged,
with a proposed framework to optimize strategies in real-world
conditions. The text also addresses research gaps, introducing a
method to reconcile diverse evaluation outcomes, and suggests
blockchain’s potential to reduce environmental impact while
enhancing coordination in SCs. The discussion demonstrates
blockchain’s practical relevance in the O&G sector, provid-
ing actionable insights and decision-making tools for industry
professionals, with a focus on scenario-based and risk-based
approaches.
Several prominent companies in the O&G industry are ac-
tively adopting BT in real-world applications. TotalEnergies
Trading & Shipping, a prominent global player in commodi-
ties and oil product negotiations, is renowned for its expertise
across key hubs spanning from Singapore to Houston, with an
established presence in Geneva and Paris [43]. The imperative to
accelerate digital trading transformation seamlessly aligns with
the strategic vision of TotalEnergies Trading & Shipping. Serv-
ing as a catalyst for digitalization, the company actively engages
in oil industry digitalization initiatives, including prominent
blockchain consortia where it maintains signiﬁcant shareholder
positions. The operationalized Digital Lab is dedicated to pi-
oneering innovative business solutions, augmenting portfolio
management capabilities, enabling real-time vessel tracking,
and identifying novel market opportunities. The integration of
BT at oil ﬁelds and reﬁneries has been instrumental in op-
timizing performance and streamlining the reconciliation of
smart contracts with vendors. Aramco’s proactive embrace of
this technology has earned recognition from Forbes as an early
adopter within the industry [44].
Shell’s blockchain strategy is underpinned by three core pil-
lars, leveraging the technology to overhaul processes, realize
cost efﬁciencies, and standardize operations across the energy
sector [45]. The company aims to discover new value opportuni-
ties in emerging markets by redeﬁning end-to-end value chains
through the adoption of BT. Emphasizing the importance of
decentralized ecosystems, Shell actively supports public net-
works tailored for enterprise use and actively participates in
a range of blockchain initiatives at varying levels of develop-
ment. Collaborating with startups, joint ventures, and industry
consortia, Shell explores novel business prospects. Signiﬁcantly,
Shell utilizes blockchain to verify the sustainability of hydrogen
sources and has launched a blockchain-based solution to expand
the use of sustainable aviation fuel. Through the trial of a
decentralized digital passport system, Shell utilizes blockchain
to improve tracking and safety management within SCs, striv-
ing to minimize costs and boost productivity. Established in
2017, the Shell Blockchain Centre of Excellence coordinates
blockchain endeavors, shares knowledge, and establishes strate-
gic alliances to navigate the evolving decentralized landscape.
The center concentrates on achieving technological advantages,
making informed technology decisions, and developing key
capabilities. Comprising conceptual, legal, and technical ex-
perts alongside blockchain developers, the team collaborates
closely with blockchain product teams embedded within Shell’s
businesses.
A. Strategic Recommendations
The sophisticated intrinsic of the O&G industry, marked
by sensitive data, diverse stakeholders, extensive paperwork,
and transaction complexities, necessitates the swift adoption
of BT in its SC operations. Preferred BT adoption strategies

were to be based on a well-balanced improvement, multiple
approaches of decision making already determined with re-
spect to efﬁciency, practicality, and risk considerations, with
optimization, statistics analysis, and ML concepts. To navigate
this terrain effectively, SC managers must meticulously evaluate
blockchain deployment strategies using comprehensive criteria
and aggregate evaluation results to arrive at informed decisions.
In this view, managers can incorporate multiple perspectives into
their assessments using advanced decision-making methods,
e.g., DEA for efﬁciency benchmarking, SAW for simple scoring,
and TOPSIS for ranking alternatives based on the ideal-solution
approach. In addition, the EM for weighting criteria guarantees
that the key critical factors are prioritized without bias. Employ-
ing an aggregation method grounded in the scenario approach
enables the integration of uncertainty and risk factors into the
decision-making process, enhancing managerial insights and
guiding strategic choices.
SC managers should thoroughly assess various blockchain
deployment strategies, considering a wide range of factors such
as coordination with SC partners, customer demands, market
conditions, and information security requirements. It is essential
to bring together evaluation ﬁndings into a cohesive summary
that supports informed, data-driven decision-making. In this
study, managers can derive rankings that stand the test of un-
certainty and adjust to changing priorities through a consensus
approach aimed at combining the outputs of these different
models by scenario and risk. Given the complex and multiparty
nature of OGSCs, fostering stronger collaboration among stake-
holdersiscrucial. BTcansigniﬁcantlyenhancetransparencyand
streamline cooperation, leading to improved operational perfor-
mance and increased trust throughout the network. Stakeholder
cooperationrequirestheestablishmentofasharedunderstanding
among them in evaluating and reaching a consensus on the
results.
Strategic decisions made by SC managers should be closely
aligned with the outcomes of these evaluations. By drawing
on insights from blockchain strategy assessments, they can
prioritize strategies that demonstrate high levels of efﬁciency
and effectiveness, ultimately improving return on investment
and supporting broader organizational objectives. The varying
results obtained from different evaluation models highlight the
importance of building consensus among stakeholders. Man-
agers should encourage collaborative decision-making to rec-
oncile differing viewpoints and develop a uniﬁed approach to
blockchain implementation, which is key to gaining organiza-
tional support and ensuring successful execution. As the O&G
industry continues to evolve, so too must its strategies. Managers
need to establish mechanisms for ongoing monitoring and feed-
back to enable timely adjustments in response to changes in the
market,advancementsintechnology,andshiftingbusinessprior-
ities. The proposed framework inherently incorporates dynamic
adaptation, enabling ﬂexible adjustments to market ﬂuctuations
and organizational strategies. Dynamic adaptation means the
framework and related parameters can be easily updated or
recalibrated as circumstances change. Effective communication
of evaluation results and recommendations is also vital for
aligning the organization around blockchain initiatives. The
proposedframeworkpresentedinFig.2canalsobeimplemented
across different time periods and with various datasets. It means
that the framework has been validated in the O&G sector in
Norway,butitsstructuredapproachisgeneralandcanbeadapted
for other industries. Based on periodic evaluations, managers
can optimize resource allocation and develop the necessary
infrastructure.
In this study, besides the integrated multimodel evaluation
framework, advanced ML techniques, such as K-means and hier-
archical clustering, are applied in order to reveal subtle similari-
tiesanddifferencesamongBTadoptionstrategiesandevaluation
methods. Within the framework for similarity assessment and
unsupervised clustering (including K-means and hierarchical
clustering), an advantage is granted to managers in that clear
visual groups can be established, distinguishing performance
levels among strategies, making top performers, for instance,
“single-use,” easily recognizable along with moderate or weaker
alternatives, and group methodologically related strategies, such
as clustering SAW and TOPSIS results. This twofold-clustering
analysis enables managers to both validate model outcomes
and make more robust and nuanced decisions. In summary,
the proposed multimodel decision-making framework provides
substantial value to managers by improving strategic decision-
making under uncertainty in the context of blockchain adoption.
Signiﬁcantly, the evaluation framework proposed here provides
managers with a comprehensive, straightforward, easy-to-adapt
evaluation of strategy as changes in the business environment oc-
cur scenario by scenario. This means that the use of multimodel
decision analytics combined with advanced clustering methods
enables higher clarity for the managers to distinguish simi-
larities and contrasts among their blockchain strategies, even
across varied perspectives for evaluation. This setup allows for
movement in the data with reassessment as market conditions,
risk proﬁles, or organizational priorities change. Hence, even
though most insights in the work stem from the Norwegian O&G
context, the modularity of the framework, combined with a data-
driven and consensus-based approach, makes it generalizable
and customizable to most industries and decision contexts. Table
D.2 in the Supplementary Material summarizes key managerial
implications.
B. Operational Recommendations
The practical relevance of our ﬁndings was further validated
through a case study in the Norwegian O&G sector. To provide
more operational recommendations for managers based on the
ﬁndings, it is essential to discuss how the proposed framework
can be implemented in practice. The decision-making models
utilized in this study offer unique advantages in evaluating
blockchain implementation strategies, ensuring a structured and
data-driven approach. Managers are recommended to take ad-
vantage of equations (A1) to (A5) to systematically determine
the relative importance of each evaluation criterion, focusing on
key aspects such as collaboration with SC partners, customer
demands, market trends, and information security. The EM is
especially well-suited for this purpose, as it allows for objective
weighting based on the variation observed in the data.

To assess different blockchain deployment strategies, a range
of models should be employed. The SEDEA model helps iden-
tify the most efﬁcient strategy while offering deeper insights
into how alternatives compare in terms of performance. The
SAW method provides an easy-to-understand initial evaluation
through the summation of weighted performance scores. The
TOPSIS method ranks alternatives based on their distance from
the ideal solution, helping to pinpoint the most favorable options.
Meanwhile, NDEA expands the analysis by evaluating efﬁ-
ciency across linked processes, offering a broader understanding
of how strategies affect the entire network. Bringing together
ﬁndings from these various models provides a more balanced
view of possible outcomes. A scenario-based analysis using (1)
to (4) effectively accounts for uncertainty and risk, enhancing
the reliability of managerial decisions. Discrepancies between
model results should be resolved through collaborative discus-
sions and computations, ensuring agreement on the selected
blockchain strategy and facilitating successful execution.
The analysis indicates that “single-use” and “localization”
strategies consistently perform best across different evaluation
models. These approaches should be prioritized for blockchain
implementation, particularly in environments with higher risks.
Balanced operational environments, particularly those priori-
tizing weighted scenario analysis, often make transformational
strategies the most suitable option. In conclusion, the study
shows that BT adoption in the Norwegian O&G industry is
guided by strategies such as “single-use” and “localization,”
which are ranked highest for effectiveness using decision-
making models such as SAW, TOPSIS, and SEDEA. These
models help assess criteria such as market pressure, collabo-
ration with SC partners, IT infrastructure, and operational costs.
The study ﬁnds that SAW and TOPSIS align most closely
with previous research, making them strong tools for strategy
evaluation. The most important challenges include MP, NCS,
LI, and CP. A consensus optimization model is proposed to
improve decision-making compared to simpler methods such
as the geometric mean. Norwegian O&G ﬁrms are advised to
adoptacollaborative,risk-based,andscenario-focusedapproach
when implementing BT.
VII. CONCLUSION
Decision-making stands as a pivotal process wielded by SC
managers, exerting a profound inﬂuence on the efﬁcacy of
SCM. Particularly in the contemporary landscape, the judi-
cious utilization of emerging technologies such as blockchain
within the SC domain assumes paramount importance. Among
the various sectors, the O&G industry emerges as a valuable
foundation for the adoption of BTs. In order to empower SC
managers with the tools necessary for effective evaluation of
blockchain deployment strategies within their SCs, the employ-
ment of decision-making models becomes imperative. Within
the framework of this work, a collection of models utilizing
principlessuchasidealandanti-idealpoints,networktheory,and
data-driven optimization was applied to systematically analyze
these strategies. The outcomes yielded by divergent evalua-
tion methodologies often confound SC managers. To mitigate
this confusion, a consensus model contingent upon risk and
scenario analysis was developed to integrate the evaluation
results obtained from the Norwegian O&G industry. The ﬁnd-
ings delineated that the transformation strategy reigns supreme
under the scenario-oriented approach, while under the most con-
servative risk containment approach, the “single-use” strategy
rises as the most effective BT implementation strategy within
the Norwegian O&G sector. Despite the promising results and
comprehensive approach of this work, some limitations must be
acknowledged.
The study primarily focused on the Norwegian O&G sec-
tor. While this industry presents a conducive environment
for blockchain applications due to its complexity and scale,
the ﬁndings may not be directly applicable to other indus-
tries without further investigation. Another limitation was the
static nature of the analysis. It did not account for the dy-
namic nature of SCs. Future research might aim to inves-
tigate the proposed decision-making models across different
industries. This would enhance the generalizability of the
ﬁndings and recommendations. On the other hand, the in-
clusion of more decision-making models [48], [49] could
provide a more comprehensive evaluation framework. Further
exploration of uncertainty and risk factors in blockchain imple-
mentation could provide deeper insights. Future research could
focus on the practical aspects of integrating blockchain with
existing technologies and infrastructure within SCs. This would
involveexamininginteroperability,scalability,andthenecessary
investments for successful implementation. By tackling these
limitations and chasing the proposed research directions, future
work can expand upon the current ﬁndings and provide more ex-
haustive, generalizable, and actionable insights for SC managers
considering BT.
